content:
NO-GO.

Specific defect: this is not a clean independent replication of the particle-placement finding if you reuse the same 38 verb+particle pairs. Fresh nouns do **not** eliminate item dependence; the shared lexical frame can still carry the effect, so “new items” here are only partially new. At best this is a partial item-extension, not an independent replication of the original effect.

The 48-frame enlargement is not itself a directional bias — if all arms scale equally, it’s just more power. But that does **not** rescue the design from the shared-pair confound.

Also, the 10 repeated pairs with different nouns create a residual clustering problem: the same pair-level bias is being sampled twice, so any apparent firewall or placement effect can still be driven by pair identity rather than a fully independent item set.

VOTE: NO-GO
